message:
I've been thinking about how much easier it is to actually *prepare* when you have a straightforward checklist instead of a vague feeling that you should probably do something. Turns out Ready.gov has a solid, no-nonsense kit-building resource that walks you through what actually matters for your household. Nothing scary—just practical stuff that takes the guesswork out of the process.

What's one thing you already keep on hand that makes you feel a little more ready?
